title: RFC: overlay dirmeta_delegate design
body:
I'm looking for some early feedback on this.  Don't worry too much about all of the details, this is just AI-guided PoC level for now to show integration with potential kernel bits.   For example, this very intentionally ignores the zstd:chunked path but that will need considered in a final version.

Mostly I want to make sure this is a reasonable approach before I start trying to clean up things and push on the kernel side.  I want to avoid investing a nontrivial amount of time fixing the kernel only to find this isn't going to fly over here.  If everything pans out I'll tackle a more stringent final change here.

For some background around this, I've written about it on the [bootc blog](https://bootc-dev.github.io/blog/2025-dec-15-blog-containers-pitfalls-of-incomplete-tar-archives/).  It's a bit long, so the tl;dr is that the handling of implied directories in tar streams is not great and causes us quite a bit of pain in bootc.  I won't reiterate here but the blog also touches on other places in the community where this struggle comes up, it is not exclusive to bootc.

Here's the basic demonstration of the problem:

```
[root@rawhide ~]# podman system reset -f
[root@rawhide ~]# /usr/bin/podman run --quiet --rm quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc ls -l /usr
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   36 Mar 17 14:21 bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    0 Jan  1  1970 games
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   16 Mar 17 14:21 i686-w64-mingw32
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   20 Mar 17 14:21 include
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  152 Mar 17 14:21 lib
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 1716 Mar 17 14:21 lib64
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   82 Mar 17 14:21 libexec
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   82 Jan  1  1970 local
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root    3 Jan  1  1970 sbin -> bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   78 Mar 17 14:21 share
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   24 Jan  1  1970 src
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root   10 Jan  1  1970 tmp -> ../var/tmp
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   16 Mar 17 14:21 x86_64-w64-mingw32
```

bootc (via ostree in this case) goes out of its way to set the mtime to 0 everywhere.  During rechunking the layers get shuffled around a bit, but the gist is that for the above, `/usr/bin` (as an example) ends up getting defined in the base layer with the correct mtime of 0.  Later layers end up including specific binaries under `/usr/bin` but none of them explicitly re-define `/usr/bin` itself.  We end up with implicit directories created via those layers that "shadow" the intended base layer metadata.  Meanwhile for things like `/usr/local` no later layers add files to that tree, so the metadata is correct since there's nothing to shadow it.

I've drafted up what I'm calling [dirmeta_delegate](https://gist.github.com/jeckersb/9abe6d08f84be987de62fb4b701c9934) for overlayfs, which is what this patch is utilizing to correct this problem.  By setting an xattr, one can instruct overlayfs to delegate directory metadata to a lower layer.  We use this to label the implied directories during unpacking. 

With a patched kernel and a podman built using a patched containers/storage:

```
[root@rawhide ~]# grep DELEGATE /boot/config-7.0.0-0.rc3.28.fc45.x86_64
CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_DIRMETA_DELEGATE=y
[root@rawhide ~]# podman system reset -f
[root@rawhide ~]# /root/bin/podman run --quiet --rm quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc ls -l /usr
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   36 Jan  1  1970 bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    0 Jan  1  1970 games
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   16 Jan  1  1970 i686-w64-mingw32
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   20 Jan  1  1970 include
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  152 Jan  1  1970 lib
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 1716 Jan  1  1970 lib64
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   82 Jan  1  1970 libexec
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   82 Jan  1  1970 local
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root    3 Jan  1  1970 sbin -> bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   78 Jan  1  1970 share
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   24 Jan  1  1970 src
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root   10 Jan  1  1970 tmp -> ../var/tmp
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   16 Jan  1  1970 x86_64-w64-mingw32
```

comments.body: This relates to https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/issues/1221 and https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/issues/737 and also I think has implications for e.g. https://github.com/jlebon/chunkah last we talked about it right?

> This relates to [opencontainers/image-spec#1221](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/issues/1221) and [opencontainers/image-spec#737](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/issues/737) and also I think has implications for e.g. https://github.com/jlebon/chunkah last we talked about it right?

Yeah this definitely has implications for chunkah.  This whole thing can be worked around by always including the full parent directory metadata (the rpm-ostree rechunker [supports that today](https://coreos.github.io/rpm-ostree/build-chunked-oci/#using---format-version)) but per the OCI image spec that's not really how it should work, and I tend to agree with the spirit of the spec.

Here's a contrived example why this distinction can be important:

Base image `foo` has `/srv` with mode `0700`.

Base image `bar` has `/srv` with mode `0755`.

Now, we have some big 100GB `blob` that we want to add at `/srv/blob`.

If I want to build derived images from both base images, ideally I have just one layer blob with the content of `/srv/blob` and that can be shared between both.  Net used storage is 100GB.  If I have to represent (contrary to spec) the full parent tree, that means distinct layers.  Now I have two layers, each with 100GB `blob` plus the tiny metadata delta.  Net used storage is now 200GB.

> In principle, I think it’s important that the layers should behave the same in all execution environments (different graph drivers / backing stores). In that sense, relying on an overlay-only feature seems problematic. (An alternative view is that most snapshot-based graph drivers like btrfs don’t have this issue at all, and that this arises because overly needs the intermediate parents to exist in the child’s diff directory structure, and that this is really a design bug in overlay. I don’t think that’s unreasonable.) So, I’d really prefer the behavior to be defined by OCI.

Agree 100% that this should be also be driven through the spec in the places where @cgwalters noted above.  This is just a piece of the puzzle so that *if* we get the spec clarified, we're already moving things in the right direction to be able to support it properly with the overlay driver.
We also need to worry, at least for a time, about older kernels. I suppose adding an xattr should be a no-op on older kernels, but just for the record.
@jlebon tagging you here explicitly since this is highly relevant to your interests as colin noted above :smile:
It's also worth emphasizing that this is not an issue for [composefs-based storage](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/119).

FWIW, chunkah today:
- doesn't reset directory mtimes to epoch 0 but instead clamps to the canonical mtime associated with the layer (in practice this means the build time of the newest RPM in that layer)
- always emits leading directory entries in the tar stream, matching rpm-ostree's `--format-version=2`

It would help clean some things up in chunkah I think if we had something like what was proposed there, but overall those dir entries are generally not that expensive relative to the size of the whole image.

> In principle, I think it’s important that the layers should behave the same in all execution environments (different graph drivers / backing stores). In that sense, relying on an overlay-only feature seems problematic. (An alternative view is that most snapshot-based graph drivers like btrfs don’t have this issue at all, and that this arises because overly needs the intermediate parents to exist in the child’s diff directory structure, and that this is really a design bug in overlay. I don’t think that’s unreasonable.) So, I’d really prefer the behavior to be defined by OCI.

I came back to this to today because I don't think I've ever played around with the btrfs backend.  It looks like the btrfs driver already behaves differently than overlay, and that (at least in this specific case) btrfs and vfs match behaviors.  None of them are what I would consider correct though:

```
[root@rawhide ~]# podman system reset -f
[root@rawhide ~]# podman --storage-driver overlay run --quiet --rm quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc ls -l /usr
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   22 Mar 24 21:31 bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    0 Jan  1  1970 games
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   16 Mar 24 21:31 i686-w64-mingw32
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   20 Mar 24 21:31 include
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  152 Mar 24 21:31 lib
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 1716 Mar 24 21:31 lib64
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   82 Mar 24 21:31 libexec
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   82 Jan  1  1970 local
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root    3 Jan  1  1970 sbin -> bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   78 Mar 24 21:31 share
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   24 Jan  1  1970 src
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root   10 Jan  1  1970 tmp -> ../var/tmp
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   16 Mar 24 21:31 x86_64-w64-mingw32
[root@rawhide ~]# podman --storage-driver overlay system reset -f
[root@rawhide ~]# podman --storage-driver btrfs run --quiet --rm quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc ls -l /usr
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 27162 Mar 24 21:32 bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root     0 Jan  1  1970 games
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    16 Jan  1  1970 i686-w64-mingw32
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    20 Jan  1  1970 include
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   782 Mar 24 21:32 lib
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 25958 Mar 24 21:32 lib64
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  1312 Mar 24 21:32 libexec
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    82 Jan  1  1970 local
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root     3 Jan  1  1970 sbin -> bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  1346 Mar 24 21:32 share
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    24 Jan  1  1970 src
lrwxrwxrwx. 2 root root    10 Jan  1  1970 tmp -> ../var/tmp
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    16 Jan  1  1970 x86_64-w64-mingw32
[root@rawhide ~]# podman --storage-driver btrfs system reset -f
[root@rawhide ~]# podman --storage-driver vfs run --quiet --rm quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc ls -l /usr
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 27162 Mar 24 21:36 bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root     0 Jan  1  1970 games
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    16 Jan  1  1970 i686-w64-mingw32
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    20 Jan  1  1970 include
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root   782 Mar 24 21:36 lib
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 25958 Mar 24 21:36 lib64
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  1312 Mar 24 21:36 libexec
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    82 Jan  1  1970 local
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root     3 Jan  1  1970 sbin -> bin
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root  1346 Mar 24 21:36 share
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    24 Jan  1  1970 src
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root    10 Jan  1  1970 tmp -> ../var/tmp
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    16 Jan  1  1970 x86_64-w64-mingw32
```

I'm not sure offhand why `i686-w64-mingw32`, `include`, and `x86_64-w64-mingw32` get the correct timestamp, but not everything.  Will need some investigation.
